content:
Of course. Here are the memory aids for the topic "Defining software quality":

```json
{
  "mnemonics": ["FURPS+: Functionality, Usability, Reliability, Performance, Supportability (+ design, implementation, interface)"],
  "keyPoints": ["Quality is conformance to requirements (fitness for purpose)", "Quality is meeting customer needs and expectations", "Quality is a multi-faceted concept (e.g., FURPS+ model)"],
  "commonMistakes": ["Focusing only on the absence of bugs (reliability) and ignoring other factors like usability", ["Equating more features (functionality) with higher quality, instead of focusing on the right features", "Assuming quality can be tested in at the end, rather than being built in throughout the development process"]
}
```
